description_long: This object is a sleek, futuristic airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply angled wings that extend outward with a slight upward dihedral. The body is predominantly light blue with subtle panel lines and surface details, giving it a modern, high-tech appearance. Two cylindrical jet engines are mounted on either side of the fuselage, positioned just below the wings, and feature metallic gray exhausts. The tail section is compact, with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer integrated into the rear fuselage. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, featuring a dark, tinted canopy that suggests an enclosed, single-seat configuration. The overall design is reminiscent of advanced urban air mobility vehicles or small, agile fighter jets.
description: A sleek, light blue airplane with sharply angled wings, twin side-mounted jet engines, and a compact tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted with a dark, enclosed canopy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, upward-angled wings, twin jet engines, and the narrow, pointed nose with the cockpit canopy visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim fuselage, the placement of the engines below the wings, and the compact tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin jet engine exhausts, the upward dihedral of the wings, and the single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the engine mounts and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and a noticeable upward dihedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under the wings, one on each side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and integrated horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visibly extended; likely retractable landing gear based on design, but not confirmed in images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a dark, streamlined canopy.
